examples.title: Mortise & Tenon
examples.description: The classic T-joint: shoulder cuts form the tenon, the pocket is mortised into the leg, one line places and cuts both.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, mortise_and_tenon

leg = Beam(45, 45, 400, color="#8a6a45")
rail = Beam(45, 45, 260, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(leg)
mortise_and_tenon(assembly, rail, leg, face="px", at=280)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Through Dovetail
examples.description: Flared tails interlock with pins at a corner — the flare (cut through the board thickness) is what locks the joint.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, through_dovetail

tails = Beam(120, 18, 220, color="#8a6a45")
pins = Beam(120, 18, 180, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(tails)
through_dovetail(assembly, tails, pins, n_tails=3)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Box (Finger) Joint
examples.description: Alternating square fingers interleave at a 90° corner — the machine-friendly cousin of the dovetail.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, box_joint

side_a = Beam(120, 18, 220, color="#8a6a45")
side_b = Beam(120, 18, 180, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(side_a)
box_joint(assembly, side_a, side_b, n_segments=7)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Dado
examples.description: A three-sided slot across the wall houses the whole shelf end — bookcase carpentry in one call.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, dado_joint

wall = Beam(18, 200, 500, color="#8a6a45")
shelf = Beam(18, 200, 260, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(wall)
dado_joint(assembly, shelf, wall, face="px", at=300, depth=6.0)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Rabbet
examples.description: A two-sided step at the board end seats the mating board flush — the standard box/frame corner.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, rabbet_joint

edge = Beam(140, 20, 300, color="#8a6a45")
board = Beam(140, 20, 240, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(edge)
rabbet_joint(assembly, board, edge)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Cross Lap
examples.description: Both beams notched half-deep, interlocking flush where they cross.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, cross_lap

a = Beam(45, 45, 400, color="#8a6a45")
b = Beam(45, 45, 400, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(a)
cross_lap(assembly, a, b, at_a=150, at_b=200)
show(assembly)

examples.title: End Lap
examples.description: The corner variant of the half-lap: both ends notched and overlapped at 90°.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, end_lap

a = Beam(45, 45, 300, color="#8a6a45")
b = Beam(45, 45, 300, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(a)
end_lap(assembly, a, b)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Mitered Butt
examples.description: Both ends cut 45° and mated into an L — no end grain visible on the outside of the corner.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, mitered_butt

a = Beam(40, 40, 260, color="#8a6a45")
b = Beam(40, 40, 260, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(a)
mitered_butt(assembly, a, b)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Doweled Butt
examples.description: A butt joint reinforced with real dowel pins: matching bores are drilled in both pieces and the pins span the interface.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, doweled_butt

wall = Beam(60, 60, 400, color="#8a6a45")
rail = Beam(60, 60, 240, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(wall)
doweled_butt(assembly, rail, wall, face="px", at=220, n_dowels=2)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Basic Butt
examples.description: Two flat pieces butted at a right angle — no shaping; glue, dowels or screws carry the load.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, butt_joint

wall = Beam(45, 45, 360, color="#8a6a45")
rail = Beam(45, 45, 220, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(wall)
butt_joint(assembly, rail, wall, face="px", at=200)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Tongue & Groove
examples.description: A full-length tongue on one board slides into a matching groove on the other — flooring and panel glue-ups.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, tongue_and_groove

board_a = Beam(100, 21, 320, color="#8a6a45")
board_b = Beam(100, 21, 320, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(board_a)
tongue_and_groove(assembly, board_a, board_b, tongue=10.0, thickness=7.0)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Sliding Dovetail
examples.description: A dovetail-profile tongue runs the whole edge: like tongue & groove but mechanically locked against pull-apart.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, sliding_dovetail

board_a = Beam(100, 24, 320, color="#8a6a45")
board_b = Beam(100, 24, 320, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(board_a)
sliding_dovetail(assembly, board_a, board_b, tail=10.0, root=8.0)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Edge Joint
examples.description: Two boards glued edge to edge into a wider panel — the placement baseline the fancier edge joints build on.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import Assembly, show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, edge_joint

board_a = Beam(100, 20, 320, color="#8a6a45")
board_b = Beam(100, 20, 320, color="#d2b48c")

assembly = Assembly()
assembly.add_component(board_a)
edge_joint(assembly, board_a, board_b)
show(assembly)

examples.title: Finger-Jointed Box
examples.description: Four boards, four box corners — the last corner closes the loop and is verified geometrically before the cuts are trusted.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, FingerSpec, Joinery

a = Beam(120, 18, 300, color="#c9a36a")
b = Beam(120, 18, 200, color="#8a6a45")
c = Beam(120, 18, 300, color="#d2b48c")
d = Beam(120, 18, 200, color="#a97c50")

j = Joinery()
spec = FingerSpec(count=6)
j.box_corner(a.end("b").inside("ny"), b.end("a").inside("ny"), spec)
j.box_corner(b.end("b").inside("ny"), c.end("a").inside("ny"), spec)
j.box_corner(c.end("b").inside("ny"), d.end("a").inside("ny"), spec)
j.box_corner(d.end("b").inside("ny"), a.end("a").inside("ny"), spec)
show(j.build(ground=a))

examples.title: Table Corner
examples.description: Two aprons tenoned into one leg at offset heights. At the same height the builder raises a collision error instead of crossing the tenons inside the leg.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, Joinery

leg = Beam(45, 45, 700, color="#8a6a45")
rail_x = Beam(45, 45, 300, color="#d2b48c")
rail_y = Beam(45, 45, 300, color="#e0cba8")

j = Joinery()
j.mortise_and_tenon(rail_x.end("b"), leg.face("px").at(650, from_="a"))
j.mortise_and_tenon(rail_y.end("b"), leg.face("py").at(610, from_="a"))
show(j.build(ground=leg))

examples.title: Mitered Frame
examples.description: Four 45° corners closing a frame; each corner declares which face turns inward, and the closing corner must land within tolerance.
examples.python:
from cadbuildr.foundation import show
from cadbuildr_projects.woodworking import Beam, Joinery

f1 = Beam(60, 20, 400, color="#c9a36a")
f2 = Beam(60, 20, 300, color="#8a6a45")
f3 = Beam(60, 20, 400, color="#d2b48c")
f4 = Beam(60, 20, 300, color="#a97c50")

j = Joinery()
for p, q in ((f1, f2), (f2, f3), (f3, f4), (f4, f1)):
    j.miter(p.end("b").inside("ny"), q.end("a").inside("ny"))
show(j.build(ground=f1))
